suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006473 protein acetylation; GO:0006338 chromatin remodeling
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: CPX-2143 contains Hpa2 and Hpa3, both of which acetylate histone H4 in vitro (Hpa3 also Hpa2). Note: GO histone-specific acetylation BP terms (GO:0043966, GO:0043968) are obsolete as of 2024 (GitHub issue #24294); GO:0006473 is the current recommended BP term. Quote: "Hpa2 and Hpa3 (from Met-19) acetylated histones and polyamines. Whereas Hpa2 acetylated histones H3 and H4 (at H3 Lys-14, H4 Lys-5, and H4 Lys-12), Hpa3 acetylated only histone H4 (at Lys-8)."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006473 protein acetylation; GO:0006338 chromatin remodeling
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Hpa2 directly acetylates histones H3 and H4 at specific lysines (H3-K14, H4-K5, H4-K12). Note: GO histone-specific BP acetylation terms are now obsolete; GO:0006473 (protein acetylation) is the current active BP term. Quote: "Hpa2 acetylated histones H3 and H4 (at H3 Lys-14, H4 Lys-5, and H4 Lys-12)." And: "Hpa2, but not Hpa3, acetylated certain small basic proteins."

Paper frames Sat1 in context of polyamine catabolism; SSAT produces acetylated polyamines that are then oxidised by downstream polyamine oxidase. Note: abstract only, full text unavailable. Quote: "the enzyme involved in mitoguazone response of the liver is the polyamine oxidase, which acts on acetylated polyamines as substrate."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0009699 phenylpropanoid biosynthetic process [NOTE: existing annotation likely incorrect]
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: AT5G07080 (OAO95042.1) is a BAHD acyltransferase using hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA (not acetyl-CoA) as acyl donor and putrescine (not spermidine) as main acyl-acceptor. The reaction produces phenolamides, not acetylated polyamines. The GO:0032918 annotation is not supported by this paper. Quote: "we successfully predicted and verified the functions of two uncharacterized Arabidopsis BAHD acyltransferases, OAO95042.1 and NP_190301.2, which use putrescine as the main acyl-acceptor." And: "BAHD acyltransferases catalyze the transfer of acyl groups from acyl-donors (Acyl-CoA) to various acyl-acceptors" in the context of "phenolamide biosynthesis."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006598 polyamine catabolic process; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Paper describes SSAT-2 in the context of the polyamine back-conversion pathway. Note: later work (PMID:15283699) showed SSAT-2 does not function as a polyamine catabolic enzyme in vivo. Quote: "In the polyamine back-conversion pathway, spermine and spermidine are first acetylated by spermidine/spermine N(1)-acetyl-transferase (SSAT-1) and then oxidized by polyamine oxidase to produce spermidine and putrescine respectively."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006598 polyamine catabolic process; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Paper uses SSAT1 as functional comparator and explicitly characterises its role in polyamine homeostasis. Quote: "Spermidine/spermine-N1-acetyltransferase (SSAT1) is a short-lived polyamine catabolic enzyme inducible by polyamines and polyamine analogues. Induction of SSAT1 plays an important role in polyamine homoeostasis, since the N1-acetylated polyamines can be excreted or oxidized by acetylpolyamine oxidase."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006520 amino acid metabolic process (positive annotation; no specific GO term exists for thialysine N-acetylation)
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: NOT annotation strongly supported. Paper additionally shows SAT2's true substrate is thialysine, warranting a positive annotation. No specific GO term exists for thialysine acetylation; GO:0006520 is the best available parent term. Quote: "A promising candidate for the physiological substrate of SSAT2 is thialysine [S-(2-aminoethyl)-L-cysteine], which is acetylated predominantly at the epsilon-amino group with Km and kcat values of 290 muM and 5.2 s-1." And: "SSAT2 should be renamed 'thialysine N(epsilon)-acetyltransferase', and may regulate this pathway."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006520 amino acid metabolic process (positive annotation); GO:0009636 response to toxic substance
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: NOT annotation fully supported. Paper shows D2023.4 preferentially N-acetylates thialysine and confers protection against its toxicity. No specific GO term exists for thialysine acetylation; GO:0006520 is the best available parent. Quote: "the C. elegans N-acetyltransferase prefers thialysine [S-(2-aminoethyl)-L-cysteine]" (based on kcat/Km). And: "heterologous expression of C. elegans N-acetyltransferase D2023.4 in Escherichia coli, which does not possess a homologous gene, results in a pronounced resistance against the anti-metabolite thialysine."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006598 polyamine catabolic process; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: CpSSAT is part of the polyamine back-conversion pathway in C. parvum. Note: abstract only, full text unavailable. Quote: "The parasite scavenges host-derived polyamines, particularly spermine, which is then converted to the lower polyamines by the combined action of spermidine/spermine N(1)-acetyltransferase (SSAT) and polyamine oxidase (PAO)."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006598 polyamine catabolic process; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Same paper as spermidine acetylation annotation for SAT2. SSAT-2 framed in context of polyamine back-conversion. Note: later work showed SSAT-2 is not a genuine polyamine catabolic enzyme in vivo (PMID:15283699). Quote: "In the polyamine back-conversion pathway, spermine and spermidine are first acetylated by spermidine/spermine N(1)-acetyl-transferase (SSAT-1) and then oxidized by polyamine oxidase to produce spermidine and putrescine respectively."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006598 polyamine catabolic process; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: CpSSAT shows preferred substrate specificity for spermine and functions in the polyamine back-conversion pathway. Note: abstract only, full text unavailable. Quote: "The parasite scavenges host-derived polyamines, particularly spermine, which is then converted to the lower polyamines by the combined action of SSAT and PAO." And: "exhibits an ordered Bi-Bi kinetics with preferred substrate specificity for spermine."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006598 polyamine catabolic process; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Same paper as other SAT2 rows. SSAT-2 shows lowest activity toward putrescine. Paper frames all substrate activity in the polyamine back-conversion pathway context. Quote: "SSAT-2 shows the preference norspermidine>spermidine=spermine>> N(1)-acetylspermine=putrescine."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006914 autophagy; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Paper explicitly connects HDAC10 and spermidine to autophagy, and situates HDAC10 activity within polyamine metabolism. Quote: "Both HDAC10 and spermidine are known to promote cellular survival through autophagy. Accordingly, this work sets a foundation for studying the chemical biology of autophagy through the structure-based design of inhibitors that may also serve as new leads for cancer chemotherapy." And: "Intracellular polyamine metabolism is regulated by reversible acetylation and dysregulated polyamine metabolism is associated with neoplastic diseases."

NOT annotation fully supported. Paper confirms HDAC6 is an acetyllysine deacetylase, not a polyamine deacetylase, supporting a positive annotation to protein deacetylation. Quote: "HDAC6 is clearly an acetyllysine deacetylase and HDAC10 is an N8-acetylspermidine deacetylase." And: "HDAC6 preferentially catalyses the deacetylation of the peptide substrate RGK(ac)-AMC with a catalytic efficiency >100-fold greater than that for the deacetylation of N8-acetylspermidine."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006914 autophagy; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Zebrafish HDAC10 (zHDAC10) was the primary structural and enzymatic model in this study. Same autophagy connection applies. Quote: "Both HDAC10 and spermidine are known to promote cellular survival through autophagy." And: "Both hHDAC10 and zHDAC10 exhibited optimal catalytic activity and specificity for the hydrolysis of N8-acetylspermidine."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006914 autophagy; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Same paper as GO:0106047 annotation for HDAC10; substrate is specifically N8-acetylspermidine. Quote: "Both HDAC10 and spermidine are known to promote cellular survival through autophagy." And: "For hHDAC10 and zHDAC10, kcat/KM=2,900+/-200 and 4,600+/-300 M-1 s-1, respectively [for N8-acetylspermidine]; in comparison, N1-acetylspermidine was a poor substrate."

NOT annotation fully supported. Same paper confirms HDAC6 deacetylates acetyllysine residues. Quote: "HDAC6 is clearly an acetyllysine deacetylase and HDAC10 is an N8-acetylspermidine deacetylase." And: "HDAC6 preferentially catalyses the deacetylation of the peptide substrate RGK(ac)-AMC with a catalytic efficiency >100-fold greater than that for the deacetylation of N8-acetylspermidine."

suggested_bp_terms: GO:0006914 autophagy; GO:0006595 polyamine metabolic process
rationale_and_supporting_quotes: Zebrafish HDAC10 was central to this study. Same autophagy and polyamine metabolic context applies. Quote: "Both HDAC10 and spermidine are known to promote cellular survival through autophagy."
